rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALMA TINA HOGAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When the petition seeks a non-pro rata distribution, it must show the computation on which the proposed distribution is based. Consents of interested beneficiaries must be filed. [LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Securities are always distributed by their shares, not their "current" market value, as that fluctuates. (Furthermore, even based on the value of the Bristol Myers Squibb stock as of 5/11/22, the net distribution of cash to each beneficiary appears to be incorrect.) The attorney "supplemented" the petition on 11/14/22 and 2/10/23. A Court-appointed fiduciary must sign and verify his own pleadings, including any supplements or amendments thereto. (2) The tangible personal property is missing from the assets report in paragraph 26 of the petition. (3) A petition for final distribution must include the complete legal description of real property. This must also be in the proposed order. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Please make a note of it. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The footer must contain the title of the paper or some clear and concise abbreviation. {CRC 2.110(b).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUANITA M. AGUILERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for failure to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/21/2022): 1) Please provide facts supporting the separate property characterization of each inventoried asset. 2) A proof that the estate cash on hand is fully insured in observance of the FDIC limit or is held in the form of investment(s) authorized by Probate Code section 9730. 3) The administrator does not appear to have acted on the creditor claim filed by the IRS. 4) The personal representative of the estate of the decedent's surviving spouse must be appointed and receive notice, unless anticipated distribution to the surviving spouse does not exceed $166,250. 5) The petition only requested a continuance of administration to 01/31/2023. The petition must be amended and renoticed to request additional time beyond 01/31/2023. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY PATANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required on the sole issue of whether this petition should be taken off calendar for failure to address the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 11/02/2022): 1) No proposed order has been received. The caption must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) Re the DE-120 form (notice of hearing) filed on 07/11/2022: i) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. New notice of a continued hearing must be given. ii) Item 1: The box must be unchecked, as this is a petition for final distribution. 3) An allegation must be made as to whether notice of administration of the estate has been given to all known or reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent. 4) An allegation must be made as to whether any public entity has a claim against the estate under Probate Code section 9201. 5) An allegation must be made as to whether any heir or beneficiary is currently or has previously been confined in any correctional facility described in Probate Code section 9202(b). 6) An allegation must be made as to whether all required income tax returns have been filed and whether income taxes, if due, have been paid by the estate. 7) An allegation must be made as to whether the petitioner has taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a). 8) The date of mailing notice of administration to the Franchise Tax Board required by Probate Code section 9202(c) must be stated. LRSF 14.35(I)(2). 9) Each asset currently held by the estate and available for distribution must be identified. CRC 7.550(b)(4). 10) A schedule of proposed distribution must be provided. The schedule must identify a specific amount of cash and description of each non-cash asset (name/number of shares for security) distributable to each beneficiary. CRC 7.651. The schedule must also address how the balance of the $50 reserve, if any, should be distributed. 11) If the assets on hand are different from those listed on the inventory and appraisal, all information required by CRC 7.550(b)(2) & (3) must be provided. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Substituted Judgment To Amend And Restate And Fund Living Trust And Execute Pour-Over Will
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared. (1) Need information if the conservatee/settlor has ever updated the 2011 Trust and if the conservatee/settlor has ever executed a Will. PC 2586. (2) Written statement from the drafting attorney to conform the presented unsigned draft of the 2011 Trust mirrors the signed copy. (3) Need information if any of the grandchildren 25 or younger. (4) Petition must list specific that are subject to transfer to the Trust. "All of her non-Trust assets" appears insufficient. (5) Need explanation if section 3.K (Release of Health Info) is necessary. (6) Proposed Order to be submitted. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DOUGLAS S. CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The second amended petition was file stamped on 2/9/23, but not actually e-filed (and available for viewing) until 2/10/23. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice. (2) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (3) Items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be answered if issue survived. (4) Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of the devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all beneficiaries of the trust must also be identified and must receive notice. (5) The first codicil was signed by the witnesses on 11/9/09? Courtesy copies of any pleadings filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 4/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/29/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No DE-147 form is on file. 2) Item 5 of the DE-121 form must be checked. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must also be attached to and served with the DE-121 form. 3) Items 2d(1) and 3e(1) of the petition: The bond waiver provision included in Article IV of the will was amended "in its entirety" by the codicil. Article IV as amended does not waive a bond. Even if the trustee of the devisee trust waives a bond, a declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate. A minimum bond ($20,000) will be required, as the proposed executor is not a resident of California. 4) Item 3f(2): Unlike the codicil, the will does not appear to be self-proving, as the witnesses did not state the place of execution required by CCP section 2015.5 & Probate Code section 8220(b). A DE-131 form must be filed. 5) The names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and their issue must be provided. 6) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

A DE-131 form must be filed. 2) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents, siblings, and their issue. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

(3) Paragraph 28 of the petition does not list all vested or contingent beneficiaries named in the trust. (4) A person claiming an interest in, or having title to or possession of, the property must be personally served notice. (5) All heirs-at-law must also receive notice with a copy of the petition. (6) Please file a copy of the vesting deed to show how title to the property is held. (7) The prayer refers to the petitioner as "Objector"? A copy of the "Supplement to Opposition" filed on 3/1/23 was just received by the assigned examiner today and has not been reviewed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELLA MAE FORD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel for Objectors to clarify if they still oppose to this amended petition as there has been no amended objection on file while Counsel for Petitioner to cure the following procedural issues: (1) Petition #8 does not set forth relations of several trust beneficiaries. (2) Petition #8 does not provide the information of the alternate executor named, Renee Underwood. (3) Notice of Petition to Administer Estate must set forth "Lost Will" being offered. Amended Notice filed on 2/7/2023; however, it failed to set forth "Lost Will" being offered. CRC 7.50 and 7.53. (4) Notice must be provided to (1) Renee Underwood. PC sec 8110(b) and (2) Attorney for objectors. CRC 7.51(b). (5) Proof of re-publication CRC 7.53 as the notice published did not set forth "Lost Will" and, Amended Petition must be re-published. (6) Notice of Remote Appearance must be provided to all persons who are entitled to notice. LRSF 14.1.D.6) PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERTA J PENTNEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/3/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) None of the other papers required to be filed have been filed. (3) The petition caption does not disclose all the relief sought. (4) Items 3e, 3f, and 3g of the petition are incomplete. (5) The will does not appear to be self-proving. Proof of Subscribing Witness must be filed. (6) Items 6 and 7 of the petition should not be checked when issue survived. (7) None of the relationships of the persons listed in item 8 of the petition is set forth. (Age is either "adult" or, for minors, the specific age.) (8) The date of death of any deceased heir must be set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] The proposed order (hard copy) and courtesy copies of all papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 4/24/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
